    42	# Feature Specification: /memory:search Runtime Bugs and Refinements
    43	
    44	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 1 -->
    45	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: spec-core | v2.2 -->
    46	
    47	---
    48	
    49	<!-- ANCHOR:metadata -->
    50	## 1. METADATA
    51	
    52	| Field | Value |
    53	|-------|-------|
    54	| **Level** | 1 |
    55	| **Priority** | P1 |
    56	| **Status** | Findings Captured |
    57	| **Created** | 2026-04-26 |
    58	| **Branch** | `main` |
    59	| **Parent Spec** | ../spec.md |
    60	| **Sibling Phases** | `001-cache-warning-hooks`, `002-memory-quality-remediation`, `003-continuity-refactor-gates`, `004-memory-save-rewrite` |
    61	<!-- /ANCHOR:metadata -->
    62	
    63	---
    64	
    65	<!-- ANCHOR:problem -->
    66	## 2. PROBLEM & PURPOSE
    67	
    68	### Problem Statement
    69	A live `/memory:search` session against the indexed-continuity runtime exposed contract violations and degraded UX across the retrieval, output rendering, and causal-graph subcommands. The empty-arguments gate fired correctly, but every downstream stage produced at least one observable defect: the intent classifier emitted `fix_bug` (confidence 0.098) for the query "Semantic Search" despite the spec mandating `understand` as the no-keyword-match fallback; the `memory_context` wrapper reported `truncated=true` and zero `results` while consuming only 2% of its 3000-3500 token budget; the assistant rendering used the explicitly-forbidden phrase "Auto-triggered memories"; `causal-stats` returned three of six valid relation types and labeled itself `health: "healthy"` while reporting `meetsTarget: false`. The root spec at `.opencode/command/memory/search.md` documents the intended behavior, but the runtime drifts from it on multiple axes.
    70	
    71	### Purpose
    72	Capture the bug catalog with reproducible runtime evidence so a subsequent remediation packet can address each cluster independently and re-verify against the same probes. This spec is intentionally findings-only — fixes belong to a follow-up implementation packet.
    73	<!-- /ANCHOR:problem -->
    74	
    75	---
    76	
    77	<!-- ANCHOR:scope -->
    78	## 3. SCOPE
    79	
    80	### In Scope
    81	- Documenting every observed defect from the conversation transcript and the live reproduction probes against `memory_context`, `memory_search`, and `memory_causal_stats`.
    82	- Cross-referencing each defect against the canonical spec at `.opencode/command/memory/search.md` to separate documented contract violations from undocumented gaps.
    83	- Clustering defects by root cause to enable independent remediation.
    84	- Recording the literal probe output as evidence so later runs can detect regression or confirm a fix.
    85	
    86	### Out of Scope
    87	- Implementing fixes (deferred to a follow-up packet referenced from `plan.md`).
    88	- Modifying the `/memory:search` command source file at `.opencode/command/memory/search.md`.
    89	- Touching the runtime MCP server source code under `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`.
    90	- Re-architecting the hybrid retrieval pipeline (graph + vector + FTS5/BM25); only the observable surface defects are catalogued.
    91	
    92	### Files to Change
    93	
    94	| File Path | Change Type | Description |
    95	|-----------|-------------|-------------|
    96	| `spec.md` | Create | This findings spec. |
    97	| `plan.md` | Create | Remediation strategy and root-cause clusters. |
    98	| `tasks.md` | Create | Per-defect work units. |
    99	| `implementation-summary.md` | Create | Placeholder until remediation lands. |
   100	| `description.json` | Create | Spec metadata for memory indexing. |
   101	| `graph-metadata.json` | Create | Graph-derived metadata for traversal. |
   102	<!-- /ANCHOR:scope -->
   103	
   104	---
   105	
   106	<!-- ANCHOR:requirements -->
   107	## 4. REQUIREMENTS
   108	
   109	### P0 — Correctness Bugs (broken contract)
   110	
   111	| ID | Requirement | Acceptance Criteria |
   112	|----|-------------|---------------------|
   113	| REQ-001 | Intent classifier MUST honor the documented no-keyword-match fallback. | For queries with no keyword hit and `confidence < 0.30`, the classifier returns `understand` (per spec §4A "Default fallback") instead of an arbitrary low-confidence intent such as `fix_bug` (currently observed at 0.098 for "Semantic Search"). |
   114	| REQ-002 | `memory_context` MUST NOT zero out results when token usage is far below budget. | Reproduction: `memory_context({input:"Semantic Search", mode:"auto"})` currently returns `meta.tokenBudgetEnforcement.{enforced:true, truncated:true, returnedResultCount:2}` with `actualTokens:71` against `budgetTokens:3000`, and `data.content[0].text` containing `{"count":0,"results":[]}`. After fix, when `actualTokens / budgetTokens < 0.50`, the result count must equal `originalResultCount`. |
   115	| REQ-003 | Output rendering MUST use canonical vocabulary from spec §4A Step 4b. | Render layer (assistant or formatter) emits "Trigger-matched spec-doc records" and "Constitutional rules" — never "Auto-triggered memories", "Triggered memories", or "Memories". |
   116	| REQ-004 | Single intent source-of-truth MUST be exposed per response. | Currently `meta.intent.type = "fix_bug"` (conf 0.098) and `data.queryIntentRouting.queryIntent = "semantic"` (conf 0.8) appear in the SAME response with different values. Define one as authoritative for downstream rendering and either remove the other or label it explicitly as a separate concern (e.g., backend-routing vs intent-classification). |
   117	
   118	### P1 — Degraded Signal (incorrect data exposed to caller)
   119	
   120	| ID | Requirement | Acceptance Criteria |
   121	|----|-------------|---------------------|
   122	| REQ-005 | `causal-stats` MUST emit all six valid relation types (zero-filled if absent). | Spec §5B lists `caused`, `enabled`, `supersedes`, `contradicts`, `derived_from`, `supports`. Runtime returns only the three with non-zero counts. After fix, `data.by_relation` always contains all six keys. |
   123	| REQ-006 | `causal-stats` health field MUST agree with `meetsTarget`. | Currently returns `health: "healthy"` AND `meetsTarget: false`. After fix, `meetsTarget=false` ⇒ `health ∈ {"degraded","below_target"}`. |
   124	| REQ-007 | `QUALITY=gap` flag MUST trigger automatic broadening before returning. | Spec §1 promises 3-tier FTS fallback (FTS5 → BM25 → Grep) on weak retrievals. Currently `avg_score:0.13`, `quality:"gap"` is reported but no broadening occurs. After fix, gap-flagged retrievals attempt at least one fallback tier before returning. |
   125	| REQ-008 | Folder-discovery MUST NOT auto-bind on weak signal. | Currently "Semantic Search" caused `folderDiscovery.specFolder = "skilled-agent-orchestration/023-sk-deep-research-creation"` (no semantic relationship). After fix, folder-binding requires either explicit `specFolder` parameter or per-token similarity above a documented threshold. |
   126	| REQ-009 | "Context quality is degraded" hint MUST be conditional on actual session state. | Currently emitted on every fresh ephemeral session. After fix, hint is suppressed when `sessionScope=="ephemeral"` and `eventCounterStart==0`. |
   127	| REQ-010 | Causal-graph edge growth MUST be balanced across relation types. | Two snapshots ~15 minutes apart show 344 new edges, ALL `supersedes`. After fix, document the autonomous backfill job, expose its scope, and add per-relation coverage targets so `caused`/`supports`/etc. do not stagnate. |
   128	| REQ-011 | Session deduplication MUST be effective across calls within a single user session. | Currently each `/memory:search` invocation gets a new ephemeral `effectiveSessionId`, so `enableDedup=true` never engages. After fix, command threads a stable session identifier (e.g., per OpenCode session) or documents the limitation explicitly. |
   129	
   130	### P2 — Refinement Opportunities
   131	
   132	| ID | Requirement | Acceptance Criteria |
   133	|----|-------------|---------------------|
   134	| REQ-012 | CocoIndex daemon health MUST be checked before relying on the vector channel. | Current behavior: command attempts CocoIndex search, fails silently when daemon is down. After fix, command emits an explicit `WARN: vector channel unavailable, lexical-only` line in the result block when daemon is unreachable. |
   135	| REQ-013 | Sub-target coverage MUST emit a remediation suggestion. | Currently `causal-stats` reports below-target coverage with no actionable hint. After fix, output includes "Top N unlinked records" or "Run X to backfill". |
   136	| REQ-014 | AskUserQuestion custom-answer routing MUST be defined. | Spec §4 offers 7 intents + "Analysis tools". A custom answer like "Semantic Search" currently becomes the QUERY (auto-detect intent) — undocumented. Either document this routing or add explicit "Code search / Explore codebase" option that routes to CocoIndex. |
   137	| REQ-015 | Trigger and constitutional channels SHOULD participate in dedup. | Currently the same 5 trigger matches resurface across calls in the same conversation; cosmetically redundant. |
   138	| REQ-016 | Intent classifier MUST be stable across paraphrased queries. | "Semantic Search" → `fix_bug`; "Find stuff related to semantic search" → `understand`. Refinement: add a stability test corpus and tune keywords or add embedding-based classification for low-confidence cases. |
   139	| REQ-017 | Naming collision between "code graph" (structural) and "causal graph" (memory) MUST be disambiguated. | Currently the startup hook says `Code Graph: empty` while `causal-stats` reports 1135 edges across 675 linked records. After fix, startup hook says "structural code graph" and `causal-stats` says "memory causal graph", or reuse a single canonical noun.|
   140	<!-- /ANCHOR:requirements -->
   141	
   142	---
   143	
   144	### Acceptance Scenarios
   145	
   146	**Given** a query with no documented intent keywords, **when** the classifier runs with confidence below 0.30, **then** it returns `understand` per spec §4A.
   147	
   148	**Given** a `memory_context` call where `actualTokens` is under 50% of `budgetTokens`, **when** the response is built, **then** `returnedResultCount` equals `originalResultCount` and `data.content` contains the actual results (not `count:0,results:[]`).
   149	
   150	**Given** a `causal-stats` call, **when** the response is built, **then** `by_relation` contains all six valid relation keys (zero-filled if absent) and `health` agrees with `meetsTarget`.
   151	
   152	**Given** an empty-results situation with trigger matches, **when** the formatter renders, **then** the heading reads "Trigger-matched spec-doc records (matched on phrase \"<keyword>\")" and never "Auto-triggered memories".
   153	
   154	---
   155	
   156	<!-- ANCHOR:success-criteria -->
   157	## 5. SUCCESS CRITERIA
   158	
   159	- **SC-001**: Every defect in §4 has a reproducible probe (command + expected literal output diff) recorded in `tasks.md`.
   160	- **SC-002**: Defects are clustered by root cause in `plan.md` so remediation can proceed cluster-by-cluster.
   161	- **SC-003**: The follow-up remediation packet, when implemented, can re-run the probes here and observe each acceptance criterion pass.
   162	- **SC-004**: Validation passes via `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh <this-folder> --strict`.
   163	<!-- /ANCHOR:success-criteria -->
   164	
   165	---
   166	
   167	<!-- ANCHOR:risks -->
   168	## 6. RISKS & DEPENDENCIES
   169	
   170	| Type | Item | Impact | Mitigation |
   171	|------|------|--------|------------|
   172	| Risk | Some defects may share a single root cause; over-counting could inflate scope. | Medium | `plan.md` clusters defects by root cause before remediation work begins. |
   173	| Risk | Probes are timestamp-bound (causal-graph edge counts grow); regression detection needs a different metric. | Low | Use coverage percent and presence-of-relation-type rather than absolute edge counts. |
   174	| Dependency | Remediation work touches `mcp_server/` source which is owned by sibling phases. | High | Coordinate with `004-memory-save-rewrite` and any active 005-memory-indexer-invariants work before landing changes. |
   175	| Dependency | CocoIndex daemon must be runnable for REQ-012 verification. | Medium | Document the start command and add a smoke test before remediation lands. |
   176	<!-- /ANCHOR:risks -->
   177	
   178	---
   179	
   180	<!-- ANCHOR:questions -->
   181	## 7. OPEN QUESTIONS
   182	
   183	- Is the truncation logic miscalculating per-result tokens, or is the wrapper over-pruning by design? Probe: log `tokenUsageSource`, per-result token estimate, and pruning decisions to identify whether estimates are inflated 30-50× (which would explain dropping at 2% reported usage).
   184	- Should intent confidence below threshold T fall back to `understand`, or surface a sentinel `unknown`? Spec says fallback is `understand`; runtime appears to skip the fallback.
   185	- Are `enabled`, `contradicts`, `derived_from` edge types implemented in the schema but unused, or absent entirely? Inspect `causal_edges` table and `memory_causal_link` enum to confirm.
   186	- Why does `health: "healthy"` co-exist with `meetsTarget: false`? Either definition is wrong, or "healthy" measures something else (orphaned edges = 0?).
   187	- Is the supersedes-only edge growth driven by an autonomous backfill job? If so, where does it live, and how can other relation types participate?
   188	
   189	### Reproduction Evidence (captured 2026-04-26)
   190	
   191	These probes are stored under §7 to keep the canonical Level 1 template structure intact while preserving the regression-detection anchor.
   192	
   193	Probe 1 — Intent classifier mis-routing:
   194	```
   195	memory_context({input:"Semantic Search", mode:"auto"})
   196	→ meta.intent.type = "fix_bug"
   197	  meta.intent.confidence = 0.0980
   198	  meta.intent.source = "auto-detected"
   199	  data.queryIntentRouting.queryIntent = "semantic"
   200	  data.queryIntentRouting.confidence = 0.8
   201	  data.queryIntentRouting.matchedKeywords = ["search"]
   202	```
   203	Spec §4A keyword table assigns NONE of {"semantic","search"} to `fix_bug`; the documented fallback is `understand`.
   204	
   205	Probe 2 — Truncation drops to zero at 2% budget:
   206	```
   207	memory_context({input:"Semantic Search", mode:"auto"})
   208	→ meta.tokenBudgetEnforcement = {budgetTokens:3000, actualTokens:71,
   209	   enforced:true, truncated:true, originalResultCount:5, returnedResultCount:2}
   210	  data.content[0].text = '{"summary":"Context truncated to fit token budget","data":{"count":0,"results":[]}, ...}'
   211	```
   212	71 / 3000 = 2.4% utilization; 5 results pruned to 0 in payload despite meta claiming 2 returned.
   213	
   214	Probe 3 — Causal-stats inconsistency:
   215	```
   216	memory_causal_stats()
   217	→ data.by_relation = {caused: 236, supersedes: 786, supports: 113}
   218	  data.health = "healthy"  data.meetsTarget = false
   219	  data.targetCoverage = "60%"  data.currentCoverage = "56.21%"
   220	```

    30	# Implementation Plan: /memory:search Runtime Remediation
    31	
    32	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 1 -->
    33	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: plan-core | v2.2 -->

    36	
    37	<!-- ANCHOR:summary -->
    38	## 1. SUMMARY
    39	
    40	### Technical Context
    41	
    42	| Aspect | Value |
    43	|--------|-------|
    44	| **Language/Stack** | TypeScript (MCP server), Bash (validators), Markdown (command spec) |
    45	| **Framework** | Spec Kit Memory MCP server, OpenCode runtime |
    46	| **Storage** | SQLite (memory_index, causal_edges) |
    47	| **Testing** | Vitest, custom probe scripts under `scripts/tests/` |
    48	
    49	### Overview
    50	
    51	`spec.md` catalogs 17 distinct defects across 4 P0, 7 P1, and 6 P2 buckets observed in `/memory:search`. Many share underlying root causes; this plan groups them into 7 clusters so a follow-up remediation packet can address each cluster independently and re-verify against the same probes captured in spec §8. This packet itself is findings-only — implementation is deferred.
    52	<!-- /ANCHOR:summary -->
    53	
    54	---
    55	
    56	<!-- ANCHOR:quality-gates -->
    57	## 2. QUALITY GATES
    58	
    59	### Definition of Ready
    60	- [x] All 17 defects mapped to REQ-001..017 in spec.md
    61	- [x] Live MCP probe evidence captured verbatim
    62	- [x] Each REQ has a falsifiable acceptance criterion
    63	- [x] Defects grouped into root-cause clusters (Cluster 1-7 in §3 below)
    64	
    65	### Definition of Done (this packet)
    66	- [x] spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, implementation-summary.md, description.json, graph-metadata.json present
    67	- [x] All placeholders filled (no `[NAME]` or `[YYYY-MM-DD]` left)
    68	- [ ] `validate.sh --strict` passes against this folder
    69	- [ ] Memory indexer surfaces this packet via `memory_search`
    70	
    71	### Definition of Done (follow-up remediation packet)
    72	- [ ] Each Cluster 1-7 addressed by a discrete code change
    73	- [ ] Re-running spec §8 probes shows acceptance criteria pass
    74	- [ ] Regression tests added for P0 fixes (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004)
    75	<!-- /ANCHOR:quality-gates -->
    76	
    77	---
    78	
    79	<!-- ANCHOR:architecture -->
    80	## 3. ARCHITECTURE
    81	
    82	### Pattern
    83	
    84	Findings-first remediation: catalog → cluster → sequence → execute. This packet performs the first three steps; the follow-up packet executes step four.
    85	
    86	### Root-Cause Clusters
    87	
    88	- **Cluster 1 — Truncation Wrapper (P0)** — REQ-002. `memory_context` budget enforcement drops `data.content` to `count:0,results:[]` while reporting `returnedResultCount > 0`. Token usage measured at 2% of budget when truncation fires. Suspect: per-result token estimation grossly inflated, OR wrapper unconditionally stub-replaces content when `truncated=true`. Change surface: `mcp_server/.../memory_context.ts` budget enforcement layer.
    89	
    90	- **Cluster 2 — Intent Classifier Drift (P0/P1)** — REQ-001, REQ-004, REQ-016. `meta.intent.type = "fix_bug"` (confidence 0.098) returned for "Semantic Search" instead of documented `understand` fallback. Same response also carries `data.queryIntentRouting.queryIntent = "semantic"` (confidence 0.8) — two classifiers, no resolution policy. Change surface: intent detection logic + threshold gate.
    91	
    92	- **Cluster 3 — Output Rendering Vocabulary (P0)** — REQ-003. Spec §4A Step 4b mandates "Trigger-matched spec-doc records" / "Constitutional rules" wording; assistant emitted forbidden "Auto-triggered memories" phrase. Change surface: server-side renderer OR stronger spec enforcement with literal forbidden-phrase list.
    93	
    94	- **Cluster 4 — Causal-Stats Output Hygiene (P1)** — REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-013. `causal-stats` emits 3 of 6 documented relation types (zero-omission), labels `health: "healthy"` while `meetsTarget: false`, no remediation hint when target missed. Change surface: `memory_causal_stats` serializer.
    95	
    96	- **Cluster 5 — State Hygiene (P1)** — REQ-009, REQ-011, REQ-015. Default ephemeral session lifecycle defeats `enableDedup`; "Context quality is degraded" hint emitted unconditionally; trigger/constitutional channels not deduped. Change surface: command harness session-id threading + server hint emitter guard.
    97	
    98	- **Cluster 6 — Folder Discovery + Channel Health (P1/P2)** — REQ-008, REQ-012, REQ-017. Folder-discovery binds on weak signal ("Semantic Search" → `skilled-agent-orchestration/023-sk-deep-research-creation`); CocoIndex daemon failure is silent; "code graph" vs "causal graph" naming collides. Change surface: similarity threshold + startup health check + naming disambiguation.
    99	
   100	- **Cluster 7 — Quality Fallback + Edge Growth (P1/P2)** — REQ-007, REQ-010, REQ-014. `QUALITY=gap` flag is informational only; spec §1 promises 3-tier FTS fallback that never activates. Causal-graph edge growth dominated by autonomous `supersedes`-only backfill (+344 in 15 min, `caused`/`supports` unchanged). AskUserQuestion custom-answer routing undocumented. Change surface: retrieval pipeline + backfill job + command spec.
   101	
   102	### Data Flow
   103	
   104	`/memory:search "<query>"` → command harness parses args → routes to `memory_context` (retrieval mode) or analysis subcommand → wrapper assembles response with `meta` + `data` + `hints` → renderer formats output block → assistant emits to user. Defects span every stage of this pipeline; clusters above map to specific stages.
   105	<!-- /ANCHOR:architecture -->
   106

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts | sed -n '430,520p;760,830p;1140,1180p;1288,1328p;1538,1584p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   430	}
   431	
   432	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   433	   2. TOKEN BUDGET ENFORCEMENT (T205)
   434	   
   435	   Enforces per-layer/per-mode token budgets by estimating the
   436	   token count of strategy results and truncating when exceeded.
   437	   Uses character-based estimation: 1 token ≈ 4 chars.
   438	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   439	
   440	/**
   441	 * T205: Enforce token budget on strategy results.
   442	 * 
   443	 * Estimates the token count of the serialized result. If over budget,
   444	 * parses embedded result arrays and removes lowest-priority items
   445	 * until within budget. Higher-scored results are preserved.
   446	 */
   447	function enforceTokenBudget(result: ContextResult, budgetTokens: number): { result: ContextResult; enforcement: TokenBudgetEnforcement } {
   448	  const serialized = JSON.stringify(result);
   449	  const actualTokens = estimateTokens(serialized);
   450	
   451	  // Under budget — no enforcement needed
   452	  if (actualTokens <= budgetTokens) {
   453	    return {
   454	      result,
   455	      enforcement: {
   456	        budgetTokens,
   457	        actualTokens,
   458	        enforced: false,
   459	        truncated: false,
   460	      }
   461	    };
   462	  }
   463	
   464	  // REQ-002 (Cluster 1) Sanity guard: when reported usage is far below budget but
   465	  // we somehow entered enforcement (e.g. estimator ran twice on already-truncated
   466	  // payload, or budget was passed in as a tiny number), short-circuit and return
   467	  // the unmodified result. This prevents the historical regression where a
   468	  // 71-token payload against a 3000-token budget was nuked to count:0,results:[].
   469	  if (budgetTokens > 0 && actualTokens / budgetTokens < 0.50) {
   470	    return {
   471	      result,
   472	      enforcement: {
   473	        budgetTokens,
   474	        actualTokens,
   475	        enforced: false,
   476	        truncated: false,
   477	      }
   478	    };
   479	  }
   480	
   481	  // Over budget — attempt to truncate embedded results
   482	  // Strategy results contain an embedded MCPResponse with content[0].text as JSON
   483	  // That JSON has a .data.results array we can truncate
   484	  const truncatedResult = { ...result };
   485	  let parseFailed = false;
   486	  let originalResultCount: number | undefined;
   487	  let returnedResultCount: number | undefined;
   488	
   489	  const fallbackToStructuredBudget = (
   490	    baseResult: ContextResult,
   491	    parseFailedInnerText?: string,
   492	    preservedResults?: Array<Record<string, unknown>>,
   493	  ): ContextResult => {
   494	    const fallbackResult = { ...baseResult } as ContextResult;
   495	    const fallbackContent = Array.isArray((fallbackResult as Record<string, unknown>).content)
   496	      ? ((fallbackResult as Record<string, unknown>).content as Array<Record<string, unknown>>)
   497	      : [];
   498	    const contentClone = fallbackContent.map((entry) => ({ ...entry }));
   499	
   500	    // REQ-002 (Cluster 1): When structural truncation already produced a non-empty
   501	    // result set, preserve it rather than silently dropping to count:0,results:[].
   502	    // The previous behaviour caused the wrapper to report `returnedResultCount > 0`
   503	    // in metadata while shipping an empty payload to the caller.
   504	    const preservedSafe: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = Array.isArray(preservedResults)
   505	      ? preservedResults
   506	      : [];
   507	
   508	    const buildPreservedCandidates = (): Array<Record<string, unknown>> => {
   509	      if (preservedSafe.length === 0) {
   510	        return [];
   511	      }
   512	      const minimalResults = preservedSafe.map((r) => ({
   513	        id: r.id,
   514	        title: r.title,
   515	        similarity: r.similarity,
   516	        specFolder: r.specFolder,
   517	        confidence: r.confidence,
   518	        importanceTier: r.importanceTier,
   519	        isConstitutional: r.isConstitutional,
   520	        metadataOnly: true,
   760	
   761	        // Recalculate actual tokens after truncation
   762	        const newSerializedTokens = estimateTokens(JSON.stringify(truncatedResult));
   763	
   764	        if (newSerializedTokens <= budgetTokens) {
   765	          return {
   766	            result: truncatedResult,
   767	            enforcement: {
   768	              budgetTokens,
   769	              actualTokens: newSerializedTokens,
   770	              enforced: true,
   771	              truncated: true,
   772	              originalResultCount,
   773	              returnedResultCount,
   774	            }
   775	          };
   776	        }
   777	
   778	        const compacted = compactStructuredResult(innerEnvelope, currentResults, contentArr);
   779	        if (compacted) {
   780	          return {
   781	            result: compacted.result,
   782	            enforcement: {
   783	              budgetTokens,
   784	              actualTokens: compacted.actualTokens,
   785	              enforced: true,
   786	              truncated: true,
   787	              originalResultCount,
   788	              returnedResultCount,
   789	            }
   790	          };
   791	        }
   792	      }
   793	    } catch {
   794	      parseFailed = true;
   795	      parseFailedInnerText = contentArr[0].text;
   796	      // JSON parse failed — fall through to structural truncation
   797	    }
   798	  }
   799	
   800	  // Fallback when parsing fails or a structured response still exceeds budget.
   801	  // Always emit valid nested JSON rather than raw character slices.
   802	  // REQ-002 (Cluster 1): Hand the fallback the survivors from structural
   803	  // truncation so it can keep them instead of silently zero-filling.
   804	  const fallbackResult = fallbackToStructuredBudget(
   805	    parseFailed ? result : truncatedResult,
   806	    parseFailedInnerText,
   807	    preservedAfterStructural,
   808	  );
   809	
   810	  const fallbackTokens = estimateTokens(JSON.stringify(fallbackResult));
   811	
   812	  // Re-extract the actual returned count from the fallback envelope so the
   813	  // metadata never advertises survivors that the payload no longer contains.
   814	  let fallbackReturnedCount = returnedResultCount;
   815	  try {
   816	    const fallbackContent = (fallbackResult as Record<string, unknown>).content as Array<{ text?: string }> | undefined;
   817	    if (fallbackContent && fallbackContent[0]?.text) {
   818	      const parsedFallback = JSON.parse(fallbackContent[0].text) as { data?: { results?: unknown[] } };
   819	      if (Array.isArray(parsedFallback?.data?.results)) {
   820	        fallbackReturnedCount = parsedFallback.data!.results!.length;
   821	      }
   822	    }
   823	  } catch {
   824	    // keep the prior returnedResultCount on parse failure
   825	  }
   826	
   827	  return {
   828	    result: fallbackResult,
   829	    enforcement: {
   830	      budgetTokens,
  1140	      }
  1141	    }
  1142	  }
  1143	
  1144	  if (!CONTEXT_MODES[effectiveMode]) {
  1145	    effectiveMode = 'focused';
  1146	  }
  1147	
  1148	  return {
  1149	    effectiveMode,
  1150	    pressureOverrideApplied,
  1151	    pressureOverrideTargetMode,
  1152	    pressureWarning,
  1153	    intentClassification: {
  1154	      detectedIntent,
  1155	      intentConfidence,
  1156	      resumeHeuristicApplied,
  1157	      source: explicitIntent ? 'explicit' : 'auto-detected',
  1158	    },
  1159	  };
  1160	}
  1161	
  1162	function maybeDiscoverSpecFolder(options: ContextOptions, args: ContextArgs): string | undefined {
  1163	  if (args.specFolder || !isFolderDiscoveryEnabled()) {
  1164	    return undefined;
  1165	  }
  1166	
  1167	  try {
  1168	    const basePaths = getSpecsBasePaths();
  1169	    const discoveredFolder = discoverSpecFolder(args.input.trim(), basePaths) || undefined;
  1170	    if (discoveredFolder) {
  1171	      (options as Record<string, unknown>).folderBoost = {
  1172	        folder: discoveredFolder,
  1173	        factor: parseFloat(process.env.SPECKIT_FOLDER_BOOST_FACTOR || '1.3'),
  1174	      };
  1175	    }
  1176	    return discoveredFolder;
  1177	  } catch (error: unknown) {
  1178	    console.error(
  1179	      '[memory-context] folder discovery failed (non-critical):',
  1180	      error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
  1288	      specFolder: discoveredFolder,
  1289	      source: 'folder-discovery',
  1290	    } : null,
  1291	    structuralRoutingNudge,
  1292	    ...telemetryMeta,
  1293	  };
  1294	}
  1295	
  1296	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1297	   7. MAIN HANDLER
  1298	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  1299	
  1300	/** Handle memory_context tool — L1 orchestration layer that routes to optimal retrieval strategy.
  1301	 * @param args - Context retrieval arguments (intent, mode, specFolder, anchors, etc.)
  1302	 * @returns MCP response with context-aware memory results
  1303	 */
  1304	async function handleMemoryContext(args: ContextArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
  1305	  const _contextStartTime = Date.now();
  1306	  const requestId = randomUUID();
  1307	
  1308	  try {
  1309	  try {
  1310	    await checkDatabaseUpdated();
  1311	  } catch (error: unknown) {
  1312	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
  1313	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
  1314	      tool: 'memory_context',
  1315	      error: `Database state check failed: ${message}`,
  1316	      code: 'E_INTERNAL',
  1317	      details: { requestId, layer: 'L1:Orchestration' },
  1318	      recovery: {
  1319	        hint: 'The memory database may be unavailable. Retry or check database connectivity.',
  1320	      },
  1321	    });
  1322	  }
  1323	
  1324	  const {
  1325	    input,
  1326	    mode: requested_mode = 'auto',
  1327	    intent: explicit_intent,
  1328	    specFolder: spec_folder,
  1538	
  1539	  // Phase C: Intent-to-profile auto-routing for memory_context.
  1540	  // Explicit caller `profile` always takes precedence; auto-detect fills in when absent.
  1541	  // Skip for 'quick' mode: quick routes through handleMemoryMatchTriggers which does not
  1542	  // support profile formatting — setting a profile there would be a no-op.
  1543	  if (!options.profile && detectedIntent && effectiveMode !== 'quick' && isIntentAutoProfileEnabled()) {
  1544	    try {
  1545	      const autoProfile = intentClassifier.getProfileForIntent(
  1546	        detectedIntent as import('../lib/search/intent-classifier.js').IntentType
  1547	      );
  1548	      if (autoProfile) {
  1549	        options.profile = autoProfile;
  1550	        console.error(`[memory-context] Intent-to-profile auto-routing: '${detectedIntent}' → profile '${autoProfile}'`);
  1551	      }
  1552	    } catch (_autoProfileErr: unknown) {
  1553	      // Auto-profile is best-effort — never breaks context retrieval
  1554	    }
  1555	  }
  1556	
  1557	  const sessionTransition = buildSessionTransitionTrace({
  1558	    previousState,
  1559	    resumedSession,
  1560	    effectiveMode,
  1561	    requestedMode: requested_mode,
  1562	    detectedIntent: detectedIntent ?? null,
  1563	    pressureOverrideApplied,
  1564	    queryHeuristicApplied: resumeHeuristicApplied,
  1565	  });
  1566	  options.sessionTransition = options.includeTrace === true ? sessionTransition : undefined;
  1567	
  1568	  const discoveredFolder = maybeDiscoverSpecFolder(options, { ...args, input: normalizedInput });
  1569	  // FIX P0: Folder discovery sets options.folderBoost for scoring only.
  1570	  // Do NOT propagate as options.specFolder — that becomes an exact-match filter
  1571	  // in vector-index-queries.ts (m.spec_folder = ?) which silently drops all
  1572	  // results when the discovered path has no indexed memories.
  1573	  const sessionStateResult = sessionManager.saveSessionState(effectiveSessionId, {
  1574	    specFolder: options.specFolder ?? discoveredFolder ?? spec_folder,
  1575	    tenantId: args.tenantId,
  1576	    userId: args.userId,
  1577	    agentId: args.agentId,
  1578	    currentTask: normalizedInput.slice(0, 500),
  1579	  });
  1580	  if (!sessionStateResult.success) {
  1581	    console.warn(
  1582	      `[memory-context] Failed to persist session identity for ${effectiveSessionId}: ${sessionStateResult.error ?? 'unknown error'}`,
  1583	    );
  1584	  }

    52	<!-- ANCHOR:what-built -->
    53	## What Was Built
    54	
    55	A findings-only spec packet documenting 17 defects in the `/memory:search` command runtime, observed during a live conversation and confirmed via direct MCP probes. Defects are organized into 4 P0 (correctness), 7 P1 (degraded signal), and 6 P2 (refinement) requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-017). The plan groups them into 7 root-cause clusters; tasks decompose each cluster into work units for a follow-up remediation packet.
    56	
    57	### Cluster 1-3 Remediation (landed in this packet on 2026-04-26)
    58	
    59	The findings packet was extended in-place to land the three P0 cluster fixes:
    60	
    61	- **Cluster 1 — Truncation Wrapper (REQ-002)**:
    62	  - Added a sanity-guard early return in `enforceTokenBudget()` so when `actualTokens / budgetTokens < 0.50` the result is returned unmodified, matching the spec acceptance criterion.
    63	  - Threaded a `preservedAfterStructural` snapshot through the structural truncation loop so when the second-pass `compactStructuredResult` cannot reach budget, the `fallbackToStructuredBudget()` candidate-state ladder now starts with a "preserve survivors" envelope (full + metadata-only) before falling through to the legacy zero-fill envelopes. Returned-count metadata is re-derived from the actual emitted payload to eliminate the historical mismatch where `returnedResultCount=2` shipped alongside `count:0,results:[]`.
    64	  - File: `mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts` (`enforceTokenBudget` and helpers, plus the call-site at the very end of the function).
    65	
    66	- **Cluster 2 — Intent Classifier Drift (REQ-001 / REQ-004 / REQ-016)**:
    67	  - Added a centroid-only confidence floor (0.30, per spec §4A "Default fallback") that fires only when the winning intent has zero keyword + zero regex-pattern evidence. Existing single-keyword classification stays at the legacy 0.08 floor, so the 80%-accuracy regression suite (T037 / T060 / C138) keeps passing while the "Semantic Search" → fix_bug bug is eliminated.
    68	  - Annotated dual-classifier output: `meta.intent` is now explicitly `classificationKind: "task-intent"` and authoritative for rendering / anchors / mode-routing; `data.queryIntentRouting` is `classificationKind: "backend-routing"` and authoritative only for channel selection. A `seeAlso` cross-pointer breaks the symmetry the original probe flagged.
    69	  - Files: `mcp_server/lib/search/intent-classifier.ts` and `mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts`.
    70	
    71	- **Cluster 3 — Output Rendering Vocabulary (REQ-003)**:
    72	  - Added a "Forbidden Phrase Enforcement" subsection to the canonical command spec at `.opencode/command/memory/search.md` §4A Step 4b. The subsection is a literal substitution table covering "Auto-triggered memories", "Triggered memories", "Constitutional memories", and the standalone "Memories" header; spells out a mandatory pre-render gate the assistant runs before emitting; and provides a regression-safe `grep -Eci` verification command.
    73	  - Source-side runtime is rendered by the assistant, so the only correct repair surface was the spec — server-side renderer was rejected to keep the change surgical.
    74	
    75	### Verification
    76	
    77	- All targeted vitest suites green: token-budget-enforcement, memory-context, handler-causal-graph, intent-classifier, intent-routing, gate-d-regression-intent-routing (200 passing tests).
    78	- Inline runtime probes via `node` against the freshly-built `dist/`:
    79	  - `classifyIntent("Semantic Search")` → `understand` (was `fix_bug` at 0.098)
    80	  - `classifyIntent("Find stuff related to semantic search")` → `understand` (REQ-016 stability)
    81	  - `classifyIntent("fix the login bug")` → `fix_bug` (single-keyword regression-safe)
    82	  - `enforceTokenBudget` under-budget → `truncated:false`, full results preserved
    83	  - `enforceTokenBudget` true over-budget → `returnedResultCount` matches `data.results.length`
    84	- `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh ... --strict` → PASSED (0 errors, 0 warnings).
    85	
    86	### Out of Scope This Packet
    87	
    88	Clusters 4-7 (P1 / P2) remain deferred per the original plan. Boundary respected: did not refactor surrounding code beyond the bug surface; did not modify the empty-arguments interactive prompt structure; did not touch the canonical command spec for anything except the Cluster 3 forbidden-phrase enforcement section.
    89	
    90	### Headline Defects
    91	
    92	- **Truncation drops results to zero at 2% budget usage** — `memory_context({input:"Semantic Search"})` reports `actualTokens:71 / budgetTokens:3000`, yet `data.content` returns `count:0,results:[]` with `truncated:true`.
    93	- **Intent classifier returns `fix_bug` for "Semantic Search"** at confidence 0.098 — spec §4A documents `understand` as the no-match fallback.
    94	- **Dual-classifier dissonance** — same response carries `meta.intent.type = "fix_bug"` (0.098) AND `data.queryIntentRouting.queryIntent = "semantic"` (0.8); no documented resolution policy.
    95	- **Vocabulary violation** — render output used "Auto-triggered memories" — explicitly forbidden by spec §4A Step 4b.
    96	- **`causal-stats` returns 3 of 6 relation types**, reports `health: "healthy"` while `meetsTarget: false`.
    97	- **Lopsided graph growth** — 344 new edges in 15 minutes, 100% `supersedes`; `caused`/`supports` stagnate.
    98	
    99	### Files Changed

   102	|------|--------|---------|
   103	| `spec.md` | Create | Bug catalog with REQ-001..017 and live probe evidence |
   104	| `plan.md` | Create | 7 root-cause clusters with change surface and verification per cluster |
   105	| `tasks.md` | Create | Findings-packet tasks (T0.1-T0.7) + deferred remediation tasks (T1-T7) |
   106	| `implementation-summary.md` | Create | This file |

   108	| `graph-metadata.json` | Create | Graph traversal metadata |

   112	
   113	<!-- ANCHOR:how-delivered -->
   114	## How It Was Delivered
   115	
   116	1. Read the canonical command spec at `.opencode/command/memory/search.md` to establish the contract.
   117	2. Reviewed the conversation transcript for observable defects.
   118	3. Ran live MCP probes (`memory_context`, `memory_causal_stats`) to confirm and expand the defect set with hard evidence.
   119	4. Cross-referenced each defect against the spec to separate contract violations from undocumented gaps.
   120	5. Clustered defects by suspected root cause to enable independent remediation.
   121	6. Captured every probe's literal output in `spec.md` §8 so regressions can be detected.
   122	<!-- /ANCHOR:how-delivered -->
   123	
   124	---
   125	
   126	<!-- ANCHOR:decisions -->
   127	## Key Decisions
   128	
   129	| Decision | Why |
   130	|----------|-----|
   131	| Findings-only packet | Remediation crosses 7 surfaces in `mcp_server/`; bundling them risks scope creep. A separate packet can sequence Phase A/B/C from `plan.md`. |
   132	| Capture probe literals in `spec.md` §8 | Future runs can diff against the recorded output to confirm fix or regression. |
   133	| Cluster by root cause, not by severity | Defects sharing a root cause (e.g., REQ-001/004/016 all flow from intent classifier) are easier to fix together. |
   134	| 17 separate REQs even when clustered | Granular IDs let tasks reference exact acceptance criteria; clustering happens in plan.md. |
   135	<!-- /ANCHOR:decisions -->
   136	
   137	---
   138	
   139	<!-- ANCHOR:verification -->
   140	## Verification
   141	
   142	| Check | Result |
   143	|-------|--------|
   144	| All 17 defects map to a REQ ID | PASS |
   145	| Each REQ has a falsifiable acceptance criterion | PASS |
   146	| Probe evidence captured verbatim | PASS — see spec §8 |
   147	| Validation via `validate.sh --strict` | PENDING — runs at packet close |
   148	| Sibling cross-references resolve | PASS — siblings are 001-cache-warning-hooks, 002-memory-quality-remediation, 003-continuity-refactor-gates, 004-memory-save-rewrite |
   149	<!-- /ANCHOR:verification -->
   150	
   151	---
   152	
   153	<!-- ANCHOR:limitations -->
   154	## Known Limitations
   155	
   156	1. **No remediation in this packet.** All 7 clusters are deferred to a follow-up packet. This packet exists to capture findings while they are fresh; consuming the catalog requires a separate code-changing packet.
   157	2. **Probes are timestamp-bound.** Causal-graph edge counts grow continuously; regression detection should use coverage percent and presence-of-relation-type rather than absolute counts.
   158	3. **Some defects may share root causes not yet identified.** Cluster 2 may be fully explained by a single classifier-threshold fix; Cluster 1 may be one line in the budget enforcement wrapper. Until the source is read, clustering is informed-guess based on observable behavior.
   159	4. **CocoIndex daemon health was not directly probed.** REQ-012 is based on the conversation transcript reporting daemon-not-running; verify the daemon's actual state before remediation.
   160	<!-- /ANCHOR:limitations -->

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:69:A live `/memory:search` session against the indexed-continuity runtime exposed contract violations and degraded UX across the retrieval, output rendering, and causal-graph subcommands. The empty-arguments gate fired correctly, but every downstream stage produced at least one observable defect: the intent classifier emitted `fix_bug` (confidence 0.098) for the query "Semantic Search" despite the spec mandating `understand` as the no-keyword-match fallback; the `memory_context` wrapper reported `truncated=true` and zero `results` while consuming only 2% of its 3000-3500 token budget; the assistant rendering used the explicitly-forbidden phrase "Auto-triggered memories"; `causal-stats` returned three of six valid relation types and labeled itself `health: "healthy"` while reporting `meetsTarget: false`. The root spec at `.opencode/command/memory/search.md` documents the intended behavior, but the runtime drifts from it on multiple axes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:81:- Documenting every observed defect from the conversation transcript and the live reproduction probes against `memory_context`, `memory_search`, and `memory_causal_stats`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:82:- Cross-referencing each defect against the canonical spec at `.opencode/command/memory/search.md` to separate documented contract violations from undocumented gaps.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:83:- Clustering defects by root cause to enable independent remediation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:113:| REQ-001 | Intent classifier MUST honor the documented no-keyword-match fallback. | For queries with no keyword hit and `confidence < 0.30`, the classifier returns `understand` (per spec §4A "Default fallback") instead of an arbitrary low-confidence intent such as `fix_bug` (currently observed at 0.098 for "Semantic Search"). |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:114:| REQ-002 | `memory_context` MUST NOT zero out results when token usage is far below budget. | Reproduction: `memory_context({input:"Semantic Search", mode:"auto"})` currently returns `meta.tokenBudgetEnforcement.{enforced:true, truncated:true, returnedResultCount:2}` with `actualTokens:71` against `budgetTokens:3000`, and `data.content[0].text` containing `{"count":0,"results":[]}`. After fix, when `actualTokens / budgetTokens < 0.50`, the result count must equal `originalResultCount`. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:115:| REQ-003 | Output rendering MUST use canonical vocabulary from spec §4A Step 4b. | Render layer (assistant or formatter) emits "Trigger-matched spec-doc records" and "Constitutional rules" — never "Auto-triggered memories", "Triggered memories", or "Memories". |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:116:| REQ-004 | Single intent source-of-truth MUST be exposed per response. | Currently `meta.intent.type = "fix_bug"` (conf 0.098) and `data.queryIntentRouting.queryIntent = "semantic"` (conf 0.8) appear in the SAME response with different values. Define one as authoritative for downstream rendering and either remove the other or label it explicitly as a separate concern (e.g., backend-routing vs intent-classification). |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:122:| REQ-005 | `causal-stats` MUST emit all six valid relation types (zero-filled if absent). | Spec §5B lists `caused`, `enabled`, `supersedes`, `contradicts`, `derived_from`, `supports`. Runtime returns only the three with non-zero counts. After fix, `data.by_relation` always contains all six keys. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:123:| REQ-006 | `causal-stats` health field MUST agree with `meetsTarget`. | Currently returns `health: "healthy"` AND `meetsTarget: false`. After fix, `meetsTarget=false` ⇒ `health ∈ {"degraded","below_target"}`. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:124:| REQ-007 | `QUALITY=gap` flag MUST trigger automatic broadening before returning. | Spec §1 promises 3-tier FTS fallback (FTS5 → BM25 → Grep) on weak retrievals. Currently `avg_score:0.13`, `quality:"gap"` is reported but no broadening occurs. After fix, gap-flagged retrievals attempt at least one fallback tier before returning. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:125:| REQ-008 | Folder-discovery MUST NOT auto-bind on weak signal. | Currently "Semantic Search" caused `folderDiscovery.specFolder = "skilled-agent-orchestration/023-sk-deep-research-creation"` (no semantic relationship). After fix, folder-binding requires either explicit `specFolder` parameter or per-token similarity above a documented threshold. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:126:| REQ-009 | "Context quality is degraded" hint MUST be conditional on actual session state. | Currently emitted on every fresh ephemeral session. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/spec.md:134:| REQ-012 | CocoIndex daemon health MUST be checked before relying on the vector channel. | Current behavior: command attempts CocoIndex search, fails silently when daemon is down. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/plan.md:88:- **Cluster 1 — Truncation Wrapper (P0)** — REQ-002. `memory_context` budget enforcement drops `data.content` to `count:0,results:[]` while reporting `returnedResultCount > 0`. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/plan.md:90:- **Cluster 2 — Intent Classifier Drift (P0/P1)** — REQ-001, REQ-004, REQ-016. `meta.intent.type = "fix_bug"` (confidence 0.098) returned for "Semantic Search" instead of documented `understand` fallback. Same response also carries `data.queryIntentRouting.queryIntent = "semantic"` (confidence 0.8) — two classifiers, no resolution policy. Change surface: intent detection logic + threshold gate.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/plan.md:92:- **Cluster 3 — Output Rendering Vocabulary (P0)** — REQ-003. Spec §4A Step 4b mandates "Trigger-matched spec-doc records" / "Constitutional rules" wording; assistant emitted forbidden "Auto-triggered memories" phrase. Change surface: server-side renderer OR stronger spec enforcement with literal forbidden-phrase list.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/plan.md:94:- **Cluster 4 — Causal-Stats Output Hygiene (P1)** — REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-013. `causal-stats` emits 3 of 6 documented relation types (zero-omission), labels `health: "healthy"` while `meetsTarget: false`, no remediation hint when target missed. Change surface: `memory_causal_stats` serializer.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/plan.md:96:- **Cluster 5 — State Hygiene (P1)** — REQ-009, REQ-011, REQ-015. Default ephemeral session lifecycle defeats `enableDedup`; "Context quality is degraded" hint emitted unconditionally; trigger/constitutional channels not deduped. Change surface: command harness session-id threading + server hint emitter guard.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/plan.md:98:- **Cluster 6 — Folder Discovery + Channel Health (P1/P2)** — REQ-008, REQ-012, REQ-017. Folder-discovery binds on weak signal ("Semantic Search" → `skilled-agent-orchestration/023-sk-deep-research-creation`); CocoIndex daemon failure is silent; "code graph" vs "causal graph" naming collides. Change surface: similarity threshold + startup health check + naming disambiguation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-continuity-memory-runtime/005-memory-search-runtime-bugs/plan.md:100:- **Cluster 7 — Quality Fallback + Edge Growth (P1/P2)** — REQ-007, REQ-010, REQ-014. `QUALITY=gap` flag is informational only; spec §1 promises 3-tier FTS fallback that never activates. Causal-graph edge growth dominated by autonomous `supersedes`-only backfill (+344 in 15 min, `caused`/`supports` unchanged). AskUserQuestion custom-answer routing undocumented. Change surface: retrieval pipeline + backfill job + command spec.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/ENV_REFERENCE.md:17:**Graph as a first-class feature family.** The `SPECKIT_GRAPH_*` variables form a dedicated feature family (see [Section 6](#6-graph) and [Section 7](#7-graph-calibration)) controlling structural code graph indexing, graph-first routing in the search pipeline, causal graph traversal, and calibration profiles. Since graph-first routing is now the default query dispatch order (Code Graph -> CocoIndex -> Memory), the graph env vars are among the most impactful configuration levers.

This tells the AI assistant that results may be incomplete rather than letting it treat sparse results as authoritative.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:941:`sqlite-vec-darwin-arm64` is an optional dependency installed automatically on Apple Silicon. On other platforms, the equivalent platform package is resolved by npm. The server degrades to non-vector behavior when sqlite-vec is unavailable. Semantic similarity quality drops until you fix the extension.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:53:> Note: When this server says "memory," it means our local indexed-continuity store — the SQLite-backed spec-doc record index that ships with this skill. It is **not** Anthropic Claude Memory (the managed product surfaced in claude.ai) and it is **not** the MCP reference `memory` server (the upstream community example). Identifiers (`memory_*` MCP tools, `memory_*` SQL tables, `memory-*.ts` handlers, `MEMORY_*` constants) are frozen by REQ-001; the disambiguation lives in operator-facing prose only.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:59:| **MCP tools** | Live registry | Core memory layers plus dedicated code graph, CocoIndex dispatch, and Skill Advisor groups |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:60:| **Search channels** | 5 core + CocoIndex bridge | Vector, FTS5, BM25, Causal Graph, Degree (+ CocoIndex semantic code search as external bridge) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:73:| **Search** | Vector similarity only | 5 core channels + CocoIndex bridge, fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (K tuned per intent) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:74:| **Routing** | No routing | Graph-first structural routing: Code Graph -> CocoIndex -> Memory; 3-tier FTS fallback when graph/semantic miss |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:82:| **Save quality** | Accept everything | 3-layer gate (structure, semantic sufficiency, duplicate) with dry-run preview |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:89:The indexed-continuity store exposes its MCP tools through 4 memory slash commands plus the borrowed recovery workflow in `/spec_kit:resume`. Dedicated code-graph, CocoIndex, and Skill Advisor tools live in the same server. Think of commands as doors into the system. Each door opens access only to the tools it needs.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:245:**Five core search channels** work together, with **CocoIndex** available as an external semantic code search bridge:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:254:| **CocoIndex** *(bridge)* | Semantic code search via vector embeddings across source files | Finding code implementations when memory channels miss; concept-first code discovery |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:256:**Graph-first routing** determines query dispatch order: structural queries route to the Code Graph first, then CocoIndex for semantic code discovery, then the 5-channel memory pipeline. This avoids forcing one search system to handle both structural relationships and semantic similarity.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:270:**Confidence truncation** cuts off results at 2x the median score gap so you never get a long tail of irrelevant items.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:272:**Evidence gap detection** (TRM Z-score) flags when retrieved memories do not adequately cover the query and suggests broadening the search.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:284:**Stage 1 -- Gather candidates** using graph-first routing: structural queries dispatch to Code Graph first, then CocoIndex for semantic code discovery, then the spec-doc record pipeline's active channels in parallel. Constitutional-tier memories are always injected regardless of score.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:331:- **LLM query reformulation** -- asks the LLM to rephrase the query more abstractly, grounding in actual knowledge base content. Reformulated hits pass through the same scope, context and quality checks as ordinary results
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:393:**Temporal contiguity** -- gives a time-proximity boost to memories created around the same time. If one memory from a Tuesday afternoon session is relevant, others from that same session probably are too. The boost fades as the time gap grows.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:428:**Verify-fix-verify loop** -- runs quality checks before saving. If the spec-doc record falls short, the system tries to fix problems automatically and checks again before storing.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:434:**Signal vocabulary expansion** -- recognizes correction signals ("actually", "wait") and preference signals ("prefer", "want") in your language, shaping quality scoring.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:468:**Result confidence scoring** -- tags each result as high, medium or low confidence using fast heuristics (no LLM needed). Checks: top-K separation, multi-channel agreement, quality score and source document structure.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:522:Research-grade infrastructure for measuring and improving search quality over time.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:524:**12-metric core computation** -- grades every query across twelve quality dimensions (MRR@1/3/10, NDCG@10, MAP and more). Together they pinpoint exactly where search is struggling, like a doctor running multiple tests instead of just asking "do you feel sick?"
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:526:**Synthetic ground truth corpus** -- 110 test questions with known correct answers in everyday language plus trick questions. Makes it possible to measure objectively whether changes improve or hurt quality. The corpus is keyed to live parent-memory IDs, so after DB rebuilds or imports you should rerun `scripts/evals/map-ground-truth-ids.ts` against the active `context-index.sqlite` before trusting ablation or reporting deltas.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:528:**Ablation study framework** -- turns off each search channel one at a time and measures quality degradation (Recall@20 delta). Identifies which components are critical.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:542:**Architecture:** CocoIndex (semantic, external MCP) finds code by concept. Code Graph (structural, this server) maps imports, calls and hierarchy. Memory (session, this server) preserves decisions. The compact-merger combines all three under a 4000-token budget for compaction injection. Code-graph source is self-contained under `code-graph/` with `handlers/`, `lib/`, `tools/`, and `tests/`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:566:**Query routing:** Structural queries (callers, imports, dependencies) go to `code_graph_query`. Semantic and concept queries go to CocoIndex (`mcp__cocoindex_code__search`). Session and memory queries go to `memory_context`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:655:Resume session with combined memory, code graph and CocoIndex status in a single call. Use when you want the detailed merged resume payload directly. The response carries freshness-aware code-graph status (`fresh`, `stale`, `empty`, `error`) instead of count-only health. Session-resume auth binds `args.sessionId` to the transport caller context from `lib/context/caller-context.ts`; mismatches are rejected by default, with `MCP_SESSION_RESUME_AUTH_MODE=permissive` available for canary rollout. For the canonical first-call recovery path on session start or after `/clear`, prefer `session_bootstrap`, and for operator-facing packet recovery prefer `/spec_kit:resume`, which reconstructs context from `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity`, then packet docs.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1006:##### `memory_causal_stats`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1075:Get LLM-oriented compact graph neighborhoods. Accepts CocoIndex search results as seeds for structural expansion. Modes: `neighborhood` (1-hop calls plus imports), `outline` (file symbols), `impact` (reverse callers). Successful responses return `status: "ok"` plus readiness/trust metadata, resolved anchors, graph context, and `data.metadata.partialOutput` so callers can see whether deadline or token-budget pressure produced partial output. When the read path determines the graph needs a full scan, the tool returns an explicit `status: "blocked"` payload with `data.blocked`, `graphAnswersOmitted`, `requiredAction: "code_graph_scan"`, and the same readiness/trust metadata instead of partial graph answers.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1082:| `seeds` | array | Seeds from CocoIndex, manual input or graph lookups |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1095:Scan the workspace for new or changed packet continuity docs and supporting generated context artifacts and add them to the index. Use after adding files manually or after a git pull. Processes three source families: constitutional rules, spec documents, and supporting generated context artifacts. Spec documents stay indexed by default; during scan they run through the save pipeline in warn-only quality mode so validation issues surface as warnings instead of silently bypassing retrieval.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:568:**Length Penalty** (REQ-008):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:677:**PI-A5: Verify-Fix-Verify Loop** (`memory-save.ts` / `quality-loop.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:678:Opt-in quality loop gated by `SPECKIT_QUALITY_LOOP`. When enabled, the save path performs 1 initial evaluation plus up to 2 immediate auto-fix retries by default. The reported `attempts` count reflects actual evaluations used, so early-break cases can stop before the configured ceiling. Accepted saves persist metadata fixes and carry rewritten body content in-memory until later hard-reject gates clear under lock. Rejected memories return structured rejection feedback instead of continuing to storage.

    33	/** Flat edge representation for API responses */
    34	export interface FlatEdge {

    36	  from: string;

    64	  derivedFrom: FlatEdge[];
    65	  supports: FlatEdge[];
    66	  allEdges: FlatEdge[];
    67	  totalEdges: number;
    68	  maxDepthReached: boolean;
    69	  truncated: boolean;
    70	  truncationLimit: number | null;
    71	}
    72	
    73	interface DriftWhyArgs {
    74	  memoryId: string | number;
    75	  maxDepth?: number;
    76	  direction?: string;
    77	  relations?: string[] | null;
    78	  includeMemoryDetails?: boolean;
    79	}
    80	
    81	interface CausalLinkArgs {
    82	  sourceId: string | number;
    83	  targetId: string | number;
    84	  relation: string;
    85	  strength?: number;
    86	  evidence?: string | null;
    87	}
    88	
    89	interface CausalStatsArgs {
    90	  _?: never;
    91	}
    92	
    93	interface CausalUnlinkArgs {
    94	  edgeId: number;
    95	}
    96	
    97	function logCausalHandlerError(tool: string, error: unknown): void {
    98	  const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    99	  console.error(`[${tool}] ${message}`);
   100	}
   101	
   102	function createSanitizedCausalError(
   103	  tool: 'memory_drift_why' | 'memory_causal_link' | 'memory_causal_stats' | 'memory_causal_unlink',
   104	  error: unknown,
   105	  code: string,
   106	  details: Record<string, unknown>,
   107	  startTime: number,
   108	  publicMessage: string,
   109	): MCPResponse {
   110	  logCausalHandlerError(tool, error);
   111	  return createMCPErrorResponse({
   112	    tool,
   113	    error: publicMessage,
   114	    code,
   115	    details,
   116	    recovery: getRecoveryHint(tool, code),
   117	    startTime,
   118	  });
   119	}
   120	
   121	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   122	   2. TREE-TO-FLAT CONVERTER
   123	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   124	
   125	/**
   126	 * Flatten a CausalChainNode tree into flat edge lists grouped by relation.
   127	 * The tree from getCausalChain() encodes parent→child relationships;
   128	 * for 'forward' direction: parent=source, child=target.
   129	 * For 'backward' direction: parent=target, child=source.
   130	 */
   131	function flattenCausalTree(
   132	  root: CausalChainNode,
   133	  maxDepth: number,
   134	  direction: 'forward' | 'backward'
   135	): FlattenedChain {
   136	  const edgeDirection = direction === 'forward' ? 'outgoing' : 'incoming';
   137	  const result: FlattenedChain = {
   138	    all: [],
   139	    by_cause: [],
   140	    by_enabled: [],
   141	    by_supersedes: [],
   142	    by_contradicts: [],
   143	    by_derived_from: [],
   144	    by_supports: [],
   145	    total_edges: 0,
   146	    max_depth_reached: false,
   147	    truncated: Boolean(root.truncated),
   148	    truncation_limit: root.truncationLimit ?? null,
   149	  };
   150	
   151	  function traverse(node: CausalChainNode): void {
   152	    for (const child of node.children) {
   153	      const edge: FlatEdge = {
   154	        id: child.edgeId ?? 0,          // T202: edge ID from storage layer
   155	        from: direction === 'forward' ? node.id : child.id,
   156	        to: direction === 'forward' ? child.id : node.id,
   157	        relation: child.relation,
   158	        strength: child.strength,
   159	        depth: child.depth,
   160	        direction: edgeDirection,
   161	      };
   162	
   163	      result.all.push(edge);
   164	
   165	      // Group by relation type
   166	      const bucket = relationBucket(child.relation);
   167	      if (bucket) {
   168	        bucket.push(edge);
   169	      }
   170	
   171	      // T006 — Only flag max_depth_reached when a node exists at the depth limit.
   172	      // Nodes at maxDepth-1 with no children are natural leaves (edges were queried).
   173	      // Nodes at maxDepth were added but never explored (traverse returned early).
   174	      if (child.depth >= maxDepth) {
   175	        result.max_depth_reached = true;
   176	      }
   177	
   178	      traverse(child);
   179	    }
   180	  }
   181	
   182	  function relationBucket(relation: string): FlatEdge[] | null {
   183	    switch (relation) {
   184	      case 'caused': return result.by_cause;
   185	      case 'enabled': return result.by_enabled;
   186	      case 'supersedes': return result.by_supersedes;
   187	      case 'contradicts': return result.by_contradicts;
   188	      case 'derived_from': return result.by_derived_from;
   189	      case 'supports': return result.by_supports;
   190	      default: return null;
   191	    }
   192	  }
   193	
   194	  traverse(root);
   195	  result.total_edges = result.all.length;
   196	  return result;
   197	}
   198	
   199	/**
   200	 * Merge two flattened chains (used for 'both' direction).
   201	 * Deduplicates edges by direction+from+to+relation key.
   202	 */
   203	function mergeFlattenedChains(a: FlattenedChain, b: FlattenedChain): FlattenedChain {
   204	  const seen = new Set<string>();
   205	  const merged: FlattenedChain = {
   206	    all: [],
   207	    by_cause: [],
   208	    by_enabled: [],
   209	    by_supersedes: [],
   210	    by_contradicts: [],
   211	    by_derived_from: [],
   212	    by_supports: [],
   213	    total_edges: 0,
   214	    max_depth_reached: a.max_depth_reached || b.max_depth_reached,
   215	    truncated: a.truncated || b.truncated,
   216	    truncation_limit: a.truncation_limit ?? b.truncation_limit ?? null,
   217	  };
   218	
   219	  function addEdge(edge: FlatEdge): void {
   220	    const key = `${edge.direction}:${edge.from}:${edge.to}:${edge.relation}`;
   221	    if (seen.has(key)) return;
   222	    seen.add(key);
   223	
   224	    merged.all.push(edge);
   225	    switch (edge.relation) {
   226	      case 'caused': merged.by_cause.push(edge); break;
   227	      case 'enabled': merged.by_enabled.push(edge); break;
   228	      case 'supersedes': merged.by_supersedes.push(edge); break;
   229	      case 'contradicts': merged.by_contradicts.push(edge); break;
   230	      case 'derived_from': merged.by_derived_from.push(edge); break;
   231	      case 'supports': merged.by_supports.push(edge); break;
   232	    }
   233	  }
   234	
   235	  for (const edge of a.all) addEdge(edge);
   236	  for (const edge of b.all) addEdge(edge);
   237	
   238	  merged.total_edges = merged.all.length;
   239	  return merged;
   240	}
   241	
   242	/**
   243	 * Map tool schema direction values to getCausalChain direction values.
   244	 * Tool schema: 'outgoing' | 'incoming' | 'both'
   245	 * getCausalChain: 'forward' | 'backward'
   246	 */
   247	function mapDirection(direction: string): 'forward' | 'backward' | 'both' {
   248	  switch (direction) {
   249	    case 'outgoing': return 'forward';
   250	    case 'forward': return 'forward';   // backward compat
   251	    case 'incoming': return 'backward';
   252	    case 'backward': return 'backward'; // backward compat
   253	    case 'both': return 'both';
   254	    default: return 'forward';
   255	  }
   256	}
   257	
   258	/**
   259	 * T203: Filter a FlattenedChain to only include edges whose relation
   260	 * is in the provided set. When relations is null/empty, returns the
   300	    truncation_limit: null,
   301	  };
   302	}
   303	
   304	function toDirectionalBuckets(chain: FlattenedChain): DirectionalBuckets {
   305	  return {
   306	    caused: chain.by_cause,
   307	    enabled: chain.by_enabled,
   308	    supersedes: chain.by_supersedes,
   309	    contradicts: chain.by_contradicts,
   310	    derivedFrom: chain.by_derived_from,
   311	    supports: chain.by_supports,
   312	    allEdges: chain.all,
   313	    totalEdges: chain.total_edges,
   314	    maxDepthReached: chain.max_depth_reached,
   315	    truncated: chain.truncated,
   316	    truncationLimit: chain.truncation_limit,
   317	  };
   318	}
   319	
   320	function formatRelationSummary(chain: FlattenedChain, label: 'incoming' | 'outgoing'): string | null {
   321	  const parts: string[] = [];
   322	
   323	  if (chain.by_cause.length > 0) parts.push(`${chain.by_cause.length} caused`);
   324	  if (chain.by_enabled.length > 0) parts.push(`${chain.by_enabled.length} enabled`);
   325	  if (chain.by_supersedes.length > 0) parts.push(`${chain.by_supersedes.length} supersedes`);
   326	  if (chain.by_contradicts.length > 0) parts.push(`${chain.by_contradicts.length} contradicts`);
   327	  if (chain.by_derived_from.length > 0) parts.push(`${chain.by_derived_from.length} derived_from`);
   328	  if (chain.by_supports.length > 0) parts.push(`${chain.by_supports.length} supports`);
   329	
   330	  if (parts.length === 0) return null;
   331	  return `${label}: ${parts.join(', ')}`;
   332	}
   333	
   334	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   335	   3. MEMORY DRIFT WHY HANDLER
   336	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   337	
   338	/** Handle memory_drift_why tool - traces causal relationships for a given memory */
   339	async function handleMemoryDriftWhy(args: DriftWhyArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   340	  const {
   341	    memoryId,

   343	    direction = 'both',
   344	    relations = null,
   345	    includeMemoryDetails = true
   346	  } = args;
   347	  // Clamp maxDepth to [1, 10] server-side
   348	  const maxDepth = Math.min(Math.max(1, Math.floor(rawMaxDepth)), 10);
   349	
   350	  const startTime = Date.now();
   351	
   352	  if (memoryId === undefined || memoryId === null) {
   353	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   354	      tool: 'memory_drift_why',
   355	      error: 'memoryId is required',
   356	      code: 'E031',
   357	      details: { param: 'memoryId' },
   358	      recovery: getRecoveryHint('memory_drift_why', 'E031'),
   359	      startTime: startTime
   360	    });
   361	  }
   362	
   363	  try {
   364	    await checkDatabaseUpdated();
   365	
   366	    vectorIndex.initializeDb();
   367	    const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
   368	    if (!db) {
   369	      return createMCPErrorResponse({
   370	        tool: 'memory_drift_why',

   372	        code: 'E020',
   373	        details: {},
   374	        recovery: getRecoveryHint('memory_drift_why', 'E020'),
   375	        startTime: startTime
   376	      });
   377	    }
   378	    causalEdges.init(db);
   379	
   380	    if (relations && Array.isArray(relations)) {

   648	      ],

   653	      'memory_causal_link',
   654	      error,
   655	      ErrorCodes.CAUSAL_GRAPH_ERROR,
   656	      { sourceId, targetId, relation },

   659	    );

   663	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   664	   4. CAUSAL GRAPH STATS HANDLER
   665	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   666	
   667	/** Handle memory_causal_stats tool - returns graph coverage and health metrics */
   668	async function handleMemoryCausalStats(_args: CausalStatsArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   669	  const startTime = Date.now();
   670	
   671	  try {
   672	    await checkDatabaseUpdated();
   673	
   674	    vectorIndex.initializeDb();
   675	    const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
   676	    if (!db) {
   677	      return createMCPErrorResponse({
   678	        tool: 'memory_causal_stats',
   679	        error: 'Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.',
   680	        code: 'E020',
   681	        details: {},
   682	        recovery: getRecoveryHint('memory_causal_stats', 'E020'),
   683	        startTime: startTime
   684	      });
   685	    }
   686	    causalEdges.init(db);
   687	
   688	    const stats = causalEdges.getGraphStats();
   689	    const orphanedEdges: CausalEdge[] = causalEdges.findOrphanedEdges();
   690	
   691	    // Compute link coverage: unique memories linked / total memories
   692	    const totalMemories = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM memory_index') as import('better-sqlite3').Statement).get() as { count: number };
   693	    const uniqueLinked = new Set<string>();
   694	
   695	    // Count unique memory IDs that appear as source or target
   696	    try {
   697	      const linkedRows = (db.prepare(
   698	        'SELECT DISTINCT source_id FROM causal_edges WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM memory_index WHERE CAST(id AS TEXT) = source_id) UNION SELECT DISTINCT target_id FROM causal_edges WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM memory_index WHERE CAST(id AS TEXT) = target_id)'
   699	      ) as import('better-sqlite3').Statement).all() as Array<{ source_id: string }>;
   700	      for (const row of linkedRows) {
   701	        uniqueLinked.add(row.source_id);
   702	      }
   703	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   704	      const message = toErrorMessage(error).toLowerCase();
   705	      if (message.includes('no such table') && message.includes('causal_edges')) {
   706	        // New/partially initialized DB where causal edges table is absent.
   707	        // Coverage remains 0 in this case.
   708	      } else {
   709	        throw error;
   710	      }
   711	    }
   712	
   713	    const safeTotalEdges = stats.totalEdges ?? 0;
   714	    const coveragePercent = totalMemories.count > 0
   715	      ? Math.round((uniqueLinked.size / totalMemories.count) * 10000) / 100
   716	      : 0;
   717	
   718	    const meetsTarget = coveragePercent >= 60;
   719	    const health = orphanedEdges.length === 0 ? 'healthy' : 'has_orphans';
   720	
   721	    const summary = `Causal graph: ${safeTotalEdges} edges, ${coveragePercent}% coverage (${health})`;
   722	
   723	    const hints: string[] = [];
   724	    if (!meetsTarget) {
   725	      hints.push(`Coverage ${coveragePercent}% below 60% target - add more causal links`);
   726	    }
   727	    if (orphanedEdges.length > 0) {
   728	      hints.push(`${orphanedEdges.length} orphaned edges detected - consider cleanup`);
   729	    }
   730	    if (stats.totalEdges === 0) {
   731	      hints.push('No causal links exist yet - use memory_causal_link to create relationships');
   732	    }
   733	
   734	    return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   735	      tool: 'memory_causal_stats',
   736	      summary,
   737	      data: {
   738	        total_edges: safeTotalEdges,
   739	        by_relation: stats.byRelation,
   740	        avg_strength: stats.avgStrength,
   741	        unique_sources: stats.uniqueSources,
   742	        unique_targets: stats.uniqueTargets,
   743	        link_coverage_percent: coveragePercent + '%',
   744	        orphanedEdges: orphanedEdges.length,
   745	        health,
   746	        targetCoverage: '60%',
   747	        currentCoverage: coveragePercent + '%',
   748	        meetsTarget: meetsTarget
   749	      },
   750	      hints,
   751	      startTime: startTime
   752	    });
   753	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   754	    return createSanitizedCausalError(
   755	      'memory_causal_stats',
   756	      error,
   757	      ErrorCodes.CAUSAL_GRAPH_ERROR,
   758	      {},
   759	      startTime,
   760	      'Causal graph statistics failed.',
